mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects strengthening economic capacity that boosts equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases produce negative or flat 5-day forward returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5% annually, invalidating the regime constraint.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and corporate revenue projections, mechanically lifting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between PAYEMS growth and 20-day S&P 500 returns remains below 0.10 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase surpassing 0.5% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and forces negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms robust domestic demand that supports earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates shows a negative or insignificant coefficient in predicting 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial output during a monetary tightening cycle signals demand destruction that outweighs rate hike benefits, producing negative S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when INDPRO falls while FEDFUNDS rises, breaking the regime-specific prediction.
